runtime.title: Film Lab stopped due to an error
runtime.hint: Refresh the page (Cmd+Shift+R). If the error persists, copy the message below.

library.title: Contact Sheets (Stykówki)
library.empty: No assets in the selected collection.
library.filmstripEmpty: No thumbnails — import photos or switch collection.
library.importMoreSub: Add photos to this catalog (RAW, JPEG, PNG…)
library.confirmClear: Remove all photos from the library? This cannot be undone.
library.exifEmpty: Select an asset in the grid.
library.exifPending: No EXIF loaded in the catalog document.

develop.canvasMainAria: Develop — global preview and image adjustments

bottomStatus.generativeStubActiveTitle: Recipe envelope includes semantic.generative_stub.v1 (no generative render yet).
bottomStatus.depthProxyActiveTitle: Depth mode — weight from brightness (luma) × brush; real depth map is a later phase.
bottomStatus.phaseCKinoActiveTitle: Projection weave and/or double exposure strength is above zero (Develop → Cinema tab).

toolbar.clipLimiterTitle: Limiter preview — soft amber/cyan bands before hard clipping (works with Clipping on)
toolbar.cmykSoftProofTitle: Approximate CMYK soft proof (non-ICC — preview only, not export)
toolbar.statusTitle: Show or hide render and quality status
toolbar.uiModeTitle: Pro — full editor. Simple — mask combine graph hidden; Layers and AI tabs hidden
toolbar.brushTitleDepth: Depth mask — paints like brush until depth map is available in preview
toolbar.copyTitle: Copy current slider and curve settings
toolbar.debugNeedImageTitle: Upload a photo to generate the diagnostics report
toolbar.batchBackendTitle: Active RAW backend for the photo batch
toolbar.saveTitle: Open export dialog (Cmd/Ctrl+E) — only when a source is loaded.
toolbar.saveDisabledAria: Save unavailable — load a photo first (upload or open from Library).

exportModal.formatDngPillTitle: Archival derivative DNG from your developed preview — not unprocessed sensor RAW. Some apps (e.g. Adobe Camera Raw) may not open this file.
exportModal.formatDngNote: This DNG is a derivative-light archive: it embeds your developed image, not raw sensor data. Compatibility varies; Adobe Camera Raw and some other viewers may reject it. Do not expect the same behavior as a camera-original DNG.
exportModal.includeMaskPng: Include local mask (grayscale PNG). With mask graph A/B: slots combined using the selected operation.
exportModal.includeBeforeAfter: Include before variant (source after crop/orientation, without HME adjustments)
exportModal.includeRecipeJson: Include recipe JSON (non-destructive settings for reproducible processing)
exportModal.lossyQualityHint: Higher values mean larger files and finer detail. PNG and TIFF stay lossless.
exportModal.doubleExposureNoPlateNote: Double exposure strength is above zero, but no second plate is loaded. The exported image will match your main grade only (no second-plate blend). Load a plate under Kino → Double exposure, or set strength to 0.
exportModal.doubleExposureBatchPlateNote: Batch export: the same second plate (if loaded) is composited on every file, scaled to each output. For a different plate per image, export one file at a time.
exportModal.doubleExposurePlateRecipeNote: The second plate is used for the blend only; it is not embedded in exported recipe JSON — keep the plate file if you need to share or reopen this look elsewhere.

social.title: Social
social.desc: Optimized for Instagram and similar (1080px), sRGB, extra output sharpening.

web.title: Web gallery
web.desc: 2048px long edge. Good balance of file size and quality for screens.

full.title: Full quality
full.desc: Original resolution. Best for archive and large prints.

exportDebug.mainPreviewAbRuntimeError: Main WebGPU preview: runtime error; fallback WebGL used.
exportDebug.mainPreviewAbRuntimeFallback: Main WebGPU preview: execution path returned fallback mode; WebGL used.
exportDebug.mainPreviewAbProbeFail: Main WebGPU preview: probe failed; fallback WebGL used.
exportDebug.mainPreviewAbFallbackActive: Main WebGPU preview: fallback to WebGL active.

sourcePanel.uploadSub: JPG · PNG · WebP · TIFF · RAW / DNG
sourcePanel.rawDecoderPending: RAW/DNG architecture is ready, but the decoder is not connected yet.

pipelineOverlays.e2eKpi: E2E KPI: median per path (31-sample window), target 16 ms.

sliderHelp.inputProfileStrength: Input profile does not use profile strength.

rightPanel.historyHelp: Full change chain: every step saved in Film Lab plus the current working state.

section.kinoSubtitle: Halation, anamorphic, gate weave, and double exposure.

rightPanel.doubleExposurePlateHint: Second plate loaded (composited at current strength).
rightPanel.doubleExposureRestoredFromCache: Second plate restored from browser storage for this source.
rightPanel.doubleExposureOpfsPersistHint: Preview uses full resolution; a scaled copy may be stored for quick reload (quota-friendly).

help.emulsionChemistryIntro: Merged before render (after ingress above): H&D response biases RGB-curve luma mix; reciprocity adds exposure trim; edge acutance stacks into Clarity.
help.emulsionToneShape: H&D response (curve luma mix bias). Fine-tune with the RGB curves luma mix slider in Color.
help.emulsionEdgeVsClarity: Edge acutance adds to Clarity in the engine merge (no separate MTF filter yet).
help.panel2TonalityLabIntro: Ingress calibration and profile blend. Only digital vs negative film is modeled for chemistry today (C‑41 orange-mask path via Negative); E‑6 / K‑14 / BW / Cineon presets are not separate engine modes yet.
help.panel2ProfileBlend: Strength blends the active film profile / look — same as overall LUT stack intensity on the preview pipeline.
help.panel2LightRolloff: Highlights & shadows: soft rolloff. Whites & blacks: push extremes toward clipping — use toolbar Clipping / Limiter preview.
help.panel2ColorTrimIntro: Saturation & vibrance: chroma trim (vibrance is gentler on lower-saturation tones than global saturation).
help.panel2LabGlowIntro: Bloom / glow; toggle Lab-accurate luminous blur for perceptual glow.
help.panel2LabFadeIntro: Fade lifts the black floor (milky shadows / lab-style fade).
help.panel4SsgIntro: SSG in this pass is driven by the grain pair: amount + crystal size. Film format and push/pull from ingress calibration influence the effective look.
help.panel5OpticsPhysicsIntro: Optical and light defects: leaks, frame style, halation/anamorphic/gate-weave and double exposure in Kino.
help.panel5KinoIntro: Kino is the focused optics pass (Panel V): halation core, anamorphic streaks, gate weave and second-plate blending.
help.panel6LegacyPrintIntro: Print-side finishing pass: vignette, leak character and frame styling shape the final analog print mood.
help.panel6LegacyPrintHint: CMYK soft proof remains available as a toolbar toggle (preview only, non-ICC).
help.panel10WorkflowQcIntro: Snapshot slots S1–S4 capture the current develop state for fast A/B style workflow checks.
help.maskStudioHybridIntro: Start with intent (Subject/Sky/Background). This creates an AI-assist mask slot and keeps the advanced controls in the existing Develop flow.
help.maskStudioHybridAdvancedHint: Advanced refinement stays in the current mask-aware sliders and graph logic — no engine fork.
help.panelJDepthExportIntro: Depth source selector for roadmap work: quick luminance estimate or ONNX model path (when configured).
help.panelJDepthExportHint: DNG variant A is available in Export. DNG B / full interop remains a separate SPIKE track.
help.orangeMaskDigitalHint: Orange-mask correction applies only in Negative film workflow.
help.rawColorimetryReload: Changing this re-decodes the RAW file (may take a moment).
help.curveDoubleClick: Double-click a point to remove a curve point.
help.curveLumaMix: 0% = RGB (more saturation), 100% = Luma (no extra saturation like Capture One).
help.hslChannelsIntro: Pick an HSL channel below: adjust saturation, hue shift, and luminance independently.
help.grainIntro: 0 = no grain. Default follows film ISO.
help.bloomLabAccurateIntro: When on (default): bloom blurs D65 Lab lightness and keeps original a/b — closer to perceptual glow. Off: legacy RGB blur.
help.randomOverlaysIntro: Each click picks another file. Shortcuts: `D` = Scratches, `L` = RAW Leak.
help.cropCommitHint: Commit crop: Enter, double-click the frame, or the center handle.
help.straightenManualArmed: Click one end of the horizon, drag to the other, release. Mouse up commits straighten.
help.straightenManualIdle: Manual mode uses Pointer Events + pointer capture and normalized geometry—no drift between frames.
help.cropStateHint: Crop state is stored non-destructively as normalized `x/y/w/h`.

footer.uploadPrompt: Upload a photo to get started

canvas.smartPreviewChip: Smart preview (WebP, OPFS) — reference detail while zoomed

recipeLayers.helpOrder: Extra exposure via HME masks (top-to-bottom order = paint order).
recipeLayers.emptyList: No layers yet — add the first one.
recipeLayers.selectOrAdd: Select or add a layer on the left.

retouch.helpIntro: Non-destructive retouch uses HME masks when scope is Masked.
retouch.healHelp: Heal mixes a soft local blur — use Masked to respect your mask stack.
retouch.maskHintEmpty: Add masks on the Masks tab for masked healing.
retouch.clonePlaceholder: Clone stamp with source picking — coming in a later iteration.
retouch.removeObjectHelp: Remove Object AI will run locally when models are wired — preview stub only.
retouch.removeObjectIdle: Idle — run stub to simulate pipeline.
retouch.removeObjectDone: Stub finished — no pixels changed yet.
retouch.offHelp: Choose a tool or stay off — Develop preview stays unchanged.

aiAutomation.introHelp: JSON instructions for the look (Develop sliders patch); batch export uses the current recipe.
aiAutomation.legendPresetHelp: One curated profile preset with explicit mapping to ingress + chemistry + grain. First shipping milestone for Epic G.
aiAutomation.legendPresetMap: Map: workflow=negative film, format=35mm, push/pull=+1 EV, orange mask=46, H&D=S-curve, reciprocity=22, edge acutance=12, grain=28/38.
aiAutomation.recomputeHelp: Regenerate AI-assist Subject/Sky masks from the crop rectangle — on-device heuristic.
aiAutomation.batchHelp: Batch uses the same recipe as the preview. Pick files, then export from the modal.
aiAutomation.batchRecomputeLabel: Batch: recompute AI masks per file
aiAutomation.generativeSectionHelp: Toggle adds reserved node `semantic.generative_stub.v1` to recipe export maskGraph. Local ONNX, no uploads — real generation is a later engine milestone.
aiAutomation.generativeIntentOffTitle: Add semantic.generative_stub.v1 to exported recipe maskGraph — no render impact yet.
aiAutomation.generativeIntentOnTitle: Turn off — remove stub node from recipe projection.

codes.soft_validate_partial_envelope: Soft validation: envelope partly outside schema
codes.generative_stub_intent_without_node: Generative: UI flag without matching mask-graph node
codes.generative_stub_node_without_intent: Generative: mask-graph node without matching UI flag
codes.recipe_apply_unrecognized_envelope: Not a recognizable recipe v1 envelope
codes.recipe_clipboard_empty_recipe_text: Clipboard does not contain recipe JSON text

patterns.maskGraphNodeIdInvalid: Graph {graphIndex}, node {nodeIndex}: missing or empty node id
patterns.layerMaskGraphNodeIdUnresolved: Layer {layerIndex}: maskGraphNodeId not found in graphs

localMask.graphSimpleHidden: In Simple mode the advanced combine graph is hidden—the engine and masks behave the same.
localMask.graphHelpPrefix: Combines two stack entries into one per-pixel weight; exposure from the
localMask.graphHelp: Combines two stack entries into one per-pixel weight; exposure from the active mask.
localMask.graphNeedBrush: Enable the local mask (toolbar: brush mode) to use the graph.
localMask.graphNeedSoloOff: Turn off Solo (S) to combine masks.
localMask.graphNeedTwoMasks: At least 2 masks are required in the stack.
localMask.graphDebugAria: Combine node diagram: mask A, operator, mask B
localMask.paintHelp: Paint on the preview: blue adds mask, red erases.
localMask.aiPresetsHelp: AI presets (MVP): quick Subject/Sky starter masks you can refine by hand.
localMask.stackEmptyHint: No masks in the stack yet.
localMask.stackEmptyBody: Create a mask with + Create mask (top) or choose AI / manual tools in the right panel.
localMask.rightRailPaletteIntro: Pick AI or a manual tool to build your first mask. The same options also open from + Create mask and from Add / Subtract on a layer.
localMask.shapeToolsFromLayerStackHint: To change or add tools, use + Create mask or Add / Subtract on the left — not layer blend modes.
localMask.maskPickerAria: Choose how to create or modify the mask
localMask.quickSelectHint: Creates a new mask slot — refine shape and range below.
localMask.depthRangeUserHint: Depth masking uses an estimated map (preview quality).
localMask.maskStudioQuickIntro: Start with AI presets, then refine — hue, depth, brush — under Advanced tools.
localMask.depthProxyHelp: Depth (P2, proxy): mask = brush × pixel brightness window (brightness ≈ stand‑in depth). Real depth map is a later engine phase.
localMask.depthMapSourceShortHint: Default: a quick brightness-based depth estimate. “Depth model” uses an optional built-in model when the app is configured for it; otherwise it matches the estimate.
localMask.depthOnnxUserEstimate: Test mode: using the brightness estimate.
localMask.depthOnnxUserFallbackShort: Depth model unavailable — using the brightness estimate.
localMask.depthMapSourceOnnxHelp: ONNX: set `VITE_FILMLAB_DEPTH_ONNX_MODEL_URL` in the build. Without a URL, or with `VITE_FILMLAB_DEPTH_ONNX_USE_LUMA_FALLBACK=1`, it matches the luminance proxy. Inference uses a Web Worker by default (`VITE_FILMLAB_DEPTH_ONNX_MAIN_THREAD_ONLY=1` forces the main thread). Input layout (NCHW / NHWC) is auto; if the depth map looks wrong, set `VITE_FILMLAB_DEPTH_ONNX_INPUT_LAYOUT` to `nchw` or `nhwc`.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackNoModelUrl: No model URL in the build — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackOrt: ONNX runtime did not load — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackFetch: Could not download the model — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackSession: Could not open the ONNX session — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackLayout: Model input shape not supported — check layout env or export.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackRun: Model run failed — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackOutput: Could not read model output — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.depthOnnxFallbackGeneric: Depth inference unavailable — using luminance as stand-in.
localMask.colorPickShiftHint: Color range: Shift+click the preview to set Hue center from that pixel.
localMask.chromaRangeHelp: Chroma min/max narrow the mask to a saturation band (HSL), together with the Hue window.
localMask.edgeBrushHelp: Edge snap boosts stamp strength on luminance edges (Sobel); recipe keeps brush strokes + edgeSensitivity.
localMask.aiLatencyLine: AI latency: last {lastMs} · avg {avgMs} · best {bestMs} · worst {worstMs}
localMask.aiLatencyNoData: AI latency: no samples (run AI Subject/Sky).
localMask.aiLatencyKpiPending: AI mask KPI: waiting for first sample.
localMask.miniMaskSoloTitle: Solo — this mask only (click again to exit)
localMask.builderRangeModeHint: Pick Luma, Hue, or Depth above. Luma/Hue sliders apply in those modes; Depth currently paints like the brush until a depth map exists.

runtimeStatus.fallbackFeatureFlagOff: GPU proxy disabled by env flag.
runtimeStatus.fallbackProxyFrameError: Proxy frame error — CPU fallback active.

renderDebug.title: Render diagnostics
renderDebug.panelDropTitle: Drop a .recipe.json or recipe envelope .json on the panel (same import as the button).
renderDebug.recipeDownloadTitle: Download recipe envelope (.recipe.json) — masks / layers / stats
renderDebug.recipeLoadTitle: Load recipe envelope from file (.recipe.json / .json) and apply to the workbench
renderDebug.recipePasteTitle: Paste recipe JSON from clipboard. Shortcut: Shift+⌘/Ctrl+V when the diagnostics panel is available. Requires HTTPS for the Clipboard API.
renderDebug.recipeCopyJsonTitle: Copy current recipe envelope (JSON) to clipboard
renderDebug.maskPayloadWorkerSemantic: Worker payload: stub node in graph
renderDebug.batchPerfCopyTitle: Copy last batch measurement (JSON) to clipboard
renderDebug.batchPerfEnableHint: Set VITE_FILMLAB_BATCH_PERF=1 and rebuild dev/preview to enable measurements.
renderDebug.profileLutPreviewTitle: VITE_FILMLAB_ENABLE_PREVIEW_LUTS — disable only with =0 (isEnvEnablePreviewLuts in runtimeEnv; filmProfiles).
renderDebug.webGpuApiTitle: WebGPU API (stage 1, diagnostics; render still WebGL)
renderDebug.webGpuDeviceTitleFallback: requestDevice + limits (device destroyed immediately; stage 1 probe)
renderDebug.webGpuMainPreviewTitle: Plan §5.1.1.3: `probeMainThreadWebGpuPreview` — buffer, 2D limit, canvas+configure, clear, WGSL+`createRenderPipeline`+triangle+`textureSample` (1×1)+`proxyWebGpuShaders.wgsl` (worker parity); after input load: downscale+`fmain` (`mainThreadWebGpuHostSourceProxyPass`); full color: `createFastPreviewRenderer` (WebGL/WebGL2). A/B flag: `VITE_FILMLAB_MAIN_PREVIEW_WEBGPU_AB=1`. Inline health: {hint}.
renderDebug.hostSabTitleBody: §5.1.1.5: SAB usually requires crossOriginIsolated (COOP+COEP). Telemetry only; production does not enforce.
renderDebug.workerApiTitle: proxyRenderWorker — separate `webGpuEnvironment` copy (compare with rows above)
renderDebug.workerWebGpuProbeTitle: WebGPU probe in `proxyRenderWorker` (same pattern as main thread; render still WebGL2)
renderDebug.canvasFormatTitle: getPreferredCanvasFormat() in the worker (WebGPU proxy after successful tryAttach)
renderDebug.workerTexLimitsTitleFallback: GPUDevice.limits in the worker (after tryAttach) — includes max texture dimensions
renderDebug.workerGlLimitsTitle: WebGL2 in the worker: MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE and MAX_3D_TEXTURE_SIZE (last WebGL proxy frame)
renderDebug.workerGlFboTitle: WebGL2 proxy: `probeWebgl2Rgba16fFboUsable` probe; FBO+blit when probe OK and `VITE_FILMLAB_FAST_FBO16F`≠0 (like fast preview). `texImage2D` source still RGBA8.
renderDebug.workerGl3dLutTitle: WebGL2 proxy: 3D LUT (profile + look) in `TEXTURE_3D` — `rgba16f`+half when 3D probe and active FBO+blit, else `rgba8`.
renderDebug.gpuInputTexTitle: Worker input texture 2D size; if smaller than the “full” size below, bilinear downscale to the GPU limit was applied.
renderDebug.gpuInputDownscaleTitle: Time to bilinearly downscale the input buffer to the 2D limit in the worker (before `renderer.render`). 0 ms = cache hit; — = full resolution fits (no CPU step).
renderDebug.proxyNominalTitle: Nominal proxy frame size (`proxyComputeSize.js` = worker). “Buffer” is the source canvas (preview) size used to compute nominal; when it differs from nominal, the worker downscales further before the rest of the pipeline.
renderDebug.proxyMatchPreviewTitle: VITE_FILMLAB_PROXY_MATCH_PREVIEW (1/true/on/yes) — same as engine: proxyMax ≥ preview buffer long edge, no second downscale in the worker (cost: heavier render during interaction).
renderDebug.proxyOutputFitTitle: When true: nominal size from computeProxySize exceeded maxTextureDimension2D — proxy output fitted (GPU/CPU when 2D limit known).
renderDebug.proxyTilesTitle: How many 2D tiles the nominal proxy size would need vs. actual output after fitting to maxTextureDimension2D (telemetry; full multi-tile render not in repo yet).
renderDebug.proxySourceTexFmtTitle: Input texture format in WebGPU proxy (rgba16float if device and API allow; else rgba8unorm)
renderDebug.lut3dWorkerTitle: 3D LUT format (profile + look) in WebGPU proxy
renderDebug.lut3dParityTitle: Compare `proxyWorkerWebGpuLut3dTexFormat` (worker) with `mainThreadWebGpuLut3dTexFormat` (main-thread probe). Separate devices/cache — expect `different` when debugging.
renderDebug.readbackRbTitle: 1×1 readback at (0,0): worker (swapchain / tile) vs main-thread probe (`rb0` in WebGPU main·preview row). Different paths, resolutions, downscales — hex need not match; row is for quick scan/regression.
renderDebug.readbackRgbTitle: 1×1 readback RGB match (worker WebGPU vs main WebGPU probe): R, G, B channels.
renderDebug.interactionKindTitle: Effective `interactionKind` in the engine (from engineAdjustments): always idle when !isAdjusting — affects fast preview, proxy, HSL/grade/calibration/curve branches. See `useFilmLabEngineAdjustments`.
renderDebug.isAdjustingTitle: `isAdjusting` in `engineAdjustments` (host → engine) — true while sliders, grading wheel, curves, straighten, crop (per panel), etc.; affects preview path and E2E (drag v2) when still true at frame time.
renderDebug.e2ePanTitle: `options.e2eIsPanning` from the host (Film Lab: pan image in view) — enables E2E v3 pointer→canvas when the user isn’t holding a slider (`isAdjusting` may be false). See `readPreviewE2ePointerContext`.
renderDebug.e2eAuxTitle: `getFilmLabE2ePointerAuxSession()` — auxiliary session (e.g. crop handle in `useFilmLabCropDrag`) when React `isAdjusting` is false; enables E2E v3 pointer→canvas. Refreshed ~5×/s with panel open.
renderDebug.e2eKbdTitle: E2E v3 session after a keyboard shortcut (zoom, keyboard pan, Before/After, auto A/K, crop Enter, etc.); `isAdjusting` may be false — same context as aux. Consumed after first frame presentation.
renderDebug.e2eSchedCanvasTitle: Time from last scheduleProgressiveRender entry to pixels written on the preview canvas. Includes rAF queue, worker, and drawing; does not measure React/props delay before schedule. Also rolling median (31 samples) per `previewE2ePath` and 16 ms KPI state.
renderDebug.e2eSchedRafTitle: Optional: VITE_FILMLAB_E2E_HOST_SCHED_RAF=1 — time from last `scheduleProgressiveRender` to first host `requestAnimationFrame` that runs preview work (fast/CPU) or sends to worker. Complements sched→canvas.
renderDebug.e2eDragCanvasTitle: Time from first isAdjusting (drag/control session start) to pixels written, only if still isAdjusting at presentation. Excludes clicks without adjustment mode.
renderDebug.e2ePointerCanvasTitle: Time from last mousedown (slider, view pan, crop handle, straighten) or keyboard intent (`markFilmLabE2eKeyboardE2eIntent`) to pixels written while the matching session runs: isAdjusting, isPanning, handle (aux), or keyboard (E2E module session).
renderDebug.e2eMedianPerPathTitle: Aggregate E2E medians per `previewE2ePath` (31-sample window): compare A/B `fast-main-webgpu-ab` vs `fast-webgl` and worker/CPU paths.
renderDebug.e2eAbMedianTitle: E2E A/B shortcut: median comparison of `fast-main-webgpu-ab` and `fast-webgl` (31 samples per path).
renderDebug.e2eFrameCostTitle: Per-frame cost (workerRenderMs / fastRenderMs / cpuPreview|full) — rolling median (31 samples) and KPI; {hint}.
renderDebug.webGpuLostTitle: {message} — worker performs one automatic reinit (reinitWebGpu message).
renderDebug.fastGlMainTitle: WebGL context, input upload (rgba8), FBO+blit, LUT atlas (rgba16f / rgba8), grading fragment precision: highp with fboRgba16f, else mediump. §5.1. Worker: “W · input (tex)”.
renderDebug.cpuNominalTitle: §5.1.1.2: `getNominalProxyRenderSize` like worker; `yes` = buffer = nominal. With `VITE_FILMLAB_CPU_PREVIEW_MATCH_NOMINAL=1`, possible 2D downscale to nominal (“↓nom” field).
renderDebug.workerGpuRenderTitle: GPU render() call only in the worker (WebGL2/WebGPU); no postMessage or main-thread compositing
renderDebug.workerCpuRenderTitle: CPU path in the worker: pixel loop and curves (buffer alloc through pixels returned); no transfer to main. “Full nominal” = same W×H as GPU tiles when VITE_FILMLAB_PROXY_OUTPUT_TILES.
renderDebug.workerCpuYieldTitle: VITE_FILMLAB_PROXY_CPU_YIELD_EVERY — every N rows of the worker CPU loop `setTimeout(0)`; empty = off. Shortcut: npm run dev:proxy-cpu-yield
renderDebug.buildWebGpuProxyTitle: VITE_FILMLAB_WEBGPU_PROXY in the built bundle (WebGPU path in the worker).
renderDebug.rawAbForceWinnerTitle: Force backend winner from last A/B
renderDebug.fastGlMainRow: Main fast · GL / in / F / LUT / sh
renderDebug.fallbackRuntimeError: Main preview A/B fallback: runtime error (WebGPU → WebGL)
renderDebug.fallbackRuntimeFallback: Main preview A/B fallback: fallback during run (WebGPU → WebGL)
renderDebug.fallbackProbeFail: Main preview A/B fallback: probe failed (WebGPU → WebGL)
renderDebug.fallbackGeneric: Main preview A/B fallback: WebGPU → WebGL

restore.title: Restore autosave?
restore.question: . Restore the session (photo and settings)?
restore.hint: “Don’t restore” removes this save from the browser.

shortcutHelp.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcutHelp.pasteSettingsTitle: Preview has no text field — Cmd/Ctrl+V without Shift (Shift enables the recipe path below).
shortcutHelp.pasteRecipeTitle: When render diagnostics is available for the current source — recipe envelope JSON from clipboard; does not replace Cmd/Ctrl+V for settings.
shortcutHelp.dropRecipeTitle: Drop a .recipe.json or recipe envelope .json onto the open render diagnostics panel (same import as Import).
shortcutHelp.maskStudioWorkspace: Masks tab (with image): overlay / brush / erase or mute
shortcutHelp.dropRecipePanel: Drop recipe on render diagnostics panel
shortcutHelp.exportSaveDialogTitle: Only when a source is loaded (same as toolbar Save). Cmd/Ctrl+E has no effect with no image.
shortcutHelp.kinoTip: Develop → right panel → Cinema: projection weave, double exposure (second plate), and film-edge frames.

meta.title: MindfulLens™ Vibe System — The right vibe and look for your photos
meta.description: MindfulLens™ Vibe System: spend less time editing and give images a warm, polished feel with a strong before/after.

cart.emptyDrawer: Your cart is empty. Explore the collection.
cart.emptyUpdate: Your cart is empty. Choose from the catalog.
cart.title: Your cart

nav.megaFooter: See profiles and quick before/after comparisons

mega.filmLabDesc: Apply film simulations in the browser
mega.liveCamDesc: Try profiles live with your phone camera
mega.matcherDesc: Drop inspiration — AI picks the best profile
mega.recipeLabDesc: Build a film recipe from scratch
mega.blendStudioDesc: Blend two film stocks into one image
mega.colorSyncDesc: Match color across 2–3 cameras in one click

hero.taglineEm: Big impact without hours of slider pushing.
hero.subtitle: Cohesive, warm, cinematic images in seconds. Compare bold before/afters and pick the vibe that fits your brand.

problem.p1: Hours at the screen and skin still feels flat while your feed lacks “soul” and doesn’t stop the scroll the way you want?
problem.p2strong: Stop guessing settings—use tightly calibrated looks.
problem.p3: One click gives your photo a warm, polished character—and a profile tuned for social.

benefits.desc: You’re not buying generic presets—you’re investing in how your work looks.
benefits.card2Desc: Images gain depth and warm tones that stop the scroll.
benefits.card3Desc: Keep one recognizable style across everything you publish.

presets.desc1: 🎯 A premium film-inspired preset system designed for creators who want consistent, cinematic results without technical complexity.
presets.desc2: Built around emotion, not settings — each preset delivers a distinct visual mood in one click.
presets.desc3:
80+ Premium Presets
Cinematic Film Looks
Portrait Skin System
Black & White Collection
Creative & Experimental Looks

social.quote2: "Film Lab is brilliant for fast turnaround posts."

security.supportDesc: Clear docs and fast help when you need it.

footer.desc: MindfulLens™ Vibe System — handcrafted profiles and tools for a cohesive, premium look.
